name: get_file
description:
Fetches the content of a specific file from a GitHub repository. Token optional for public repos; required for private repos.
Use when: user wants to read a known file from a GitHub repo by its path — e.g. 'show me the README', 'get the handler.py from owner/repo'.
Scope: remote GitHub file fetch only. Requires knowing the exact file path.
Not for: searching for files by pattern (use search_code), local file reading (use standard file tools), or repo structure overview (use get_repo_info).

file_path.description: Path to the file within the repository. Example: 'README.md', 'src/handler.py'

ref.description: Branch, tag, or commit SHA to fetch from. Default: repo's default branch (usually 'main' or 'master').
